title: Tragacantha strictifolia
taxonomy: Tragacantha strictifolia belongs to the family Fabaceae, which is one of the largest and most diverse families of flowering plants. This plant is a type of legume and is commonly found in various parts of the world. The classification of Tragacantha strictifolia also includes its placement within the subfamily Faboideae and tribe Genisteae. At the genus level, it is classified as Tragacantha, which is a group of thorny plants. The species strictifolia is a specific identifier within the genus, highlighting its unique characteristics.

intro: Tragacantha strictifolia, also known as Astragalus strictifolius, is a species of legume plant in the pea family that is native to the mountainous regions of the Tibetan Plateau and surrounding areas. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immune-boosting properties, which may help to prevent or alleviate conditions such as respiratory infections, cardiovascular disease, and cancer. The bioactive constituents of Tragacantha strictifolia include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Tibetan medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tea, capsule, or tincture, and is used to treat a range of health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory problems.
